items.headline: City Addresses Affordable Housing Loss When Subsidies End
items.summary: The City Council deferred a discussion about protecting affordable housing restrictions when project-based subsidy contracts expire or properties face foreclosure. This affects existing affordable units in San José that could revert to market-rate housing.

items.headline: FIFA World Cup 2026: Santa Clara approves giant stadium signs
items.summary: The city authorized temporary supergraphic signs on building surfaces near Levi's Stadium to promote FIFA World Cup 2026 events. This marks a visible change to the cityscape during the tournament period, which will be the first World Cup held in the United States since 1994.

items.headline: Milpitas updates development fees and user charges for FY 2026-27
items.summary: The City Council approved new development impact fees and utility user charges for the fiscal year starting July 1, 2026. These changes affect costs for new construction projects and utility bills for residents and businesses.

items.headline: Santa Clara County presents homelessness prevention plan to Milpitas
items.summary: County officials presented the Community Plan to End Homelessness, outlining regional strategies that will impact how Milpitas addresses homelessness and coordinates with neighboring jurisdictions on services.

items.headline: Comm Hill Phase 3.1 development brings 4 new homes to Altino Boulevard
items.summary: Four single-family homes are under construction as part of the Comm Hill Phase 3.1 project along Altino Boulevard (lots 19-23), with prices ranging from $487k to $573k. This represents continued residential infill development in the area.

items.headline: City Council Hosts Building Permitting Guide Presentation
items.summary: A presentation titled "From Ideation to Occupancy" covered San José's building permitting process, timelines, and coordination between departments. This could help residents and developers understand the path to getting projects approved in the city.

items.headline: Sustainability Commission updates climate action plan through 2028
items.summary: The city's Sustainability Commission reviewed progress on its Climate Action Playbook with a focus on FY 2025-26 goals and the 2028 game plan. The commission also heard a presentation on edible food recovery efforts in Sunnyvale.

items.headline: Major rezoning proposed for 70-unit residential tower on Webster Street
items.summary: The City Council is considering a request to rezone 910 Webster Street from RM-20 to Planned Community zoning to allow construction of a seven-story building with 70 residential units. This represents a significant density increase for the property and will require community input during the prescreening process.
